name: Souffle Glace Aux Framboises
recipeInstructions:
1. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper or butter and flour it.
2. Trace 2 circles exactly the diameter of an 8-inch souffle dish onto parchment paper.
3. Fold the parchment paper in half lengthwise, lightly oil one side, wrap around the souffle dish extending 3 inches above the top, and secure with masking tape.
4. Preheat the oven to 200°F.
5. Beat egg whites until they form stiff peaks.
6. Stir ground almonds and sugar together and gently fold into beaten egg whites.
7. Transfer mixture to a pastry bag fitted with a round 1/2-inch tip.
8. Form two 8-inch circles, 1/4 inch thick, starting in the center and spiraling outward.
9. Bake until crisp, about 1 1/2 hours. Cool on a rack.
1. Choose a few perfect raspberries for garnish and set aside.
2. Puree remaining raspberries in a processor or blender until very smooth.
3. Transfer 2 tablespoons of puree with seeds to a measuring cup. Strain the remaining puree into a mixing bowl, removing all seeds.
4. Add enough strained puree to the 2 tablespoons to measure 1/2 cup. Set aside.
5. Chill the remaining strained puree, which will be used as sauce.
1. In a small heavy saucepan, combine sugar, water, and corn syrup.
2. Bring slowly to boil over medium heat, stirring occasionally and brushing down any sugar crystals from sides of the pan with a brush dipped in cold water.
3. Raise heat slightly and cook until the temperature of the syrup reaches 240°F (soft ball stage).
4. While the syrup is cooking, beat 3 egg whites in a mixer until stiff.
5. As soon as the syrup reaches 240°F, lower the mixer speed to slow and pour hot syrup onto beaten whites in a very thin stream.
6. When all of the syrup has been incorporated, raise the mixer speed and beat the meringue until cool.
7. Stir together the reserved raspberry puree and lemon juice and fold into the meringue until nearly blended.
8. Whip cream until stiff and fold into the meringue until blended. Do not overmix.
1. Spread a 3/4-inch layer of mousse in the prepared souffle dish.
2. Carefully peel off the paper from a meringue layer and place it on top.
3. Repeat, adding another 3/4-inch layer of mousse, then the remaining meringue layer.
4. Top with the remaining mousse, smoothing gently with a spatula.
5. Freeze at least 3 hours.
1. Carefully remove the paper collar.
2. Smooth the exposed edge of the souffle with a metal spatula if necessary.
3. Use a knife blade or long metal spatula to score shallow crisscross patterns in the top of the souffle.
4. Garnish with reserved raspberries.
5. Serve in wedges, spooning a little chilled raspberry puree around each portion and serving the remainder separately.
